steps.title: Put the equation in standard form
steps.content:
Start with
$$
x(11x+25)+c=4
$$
Expand and move all terms to one side:
$$
11x^2+25x+(c-4)=0
$$

steps.title: Match the quadratic formula
steps.content: The denominator in the quadratic formula is $2(11)=22$, matching the given solution. Therefore the expression under the radical must be $185$.

steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content: Set the discriminant equal to $185$: $$ 25^2-4(11)(c-4)=185 $$ $$ 625-44c+176=185 $$ $$ 801-44c=185 $$ $$ c=14 $$ The answer is A.
